data.title: AZZ reiterates $1.625B-$1.725B sales guidance as infrastructure tailwinds drive Metal Coatings growth
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: AZZ Inc. (AZZ) Q2 2026

Management View

  • Thomas Ferguson, President and CEO, stated that "we delivered solid second quarter results. Total sales increased by 2%, adjusted earnings per share rose 13.1% and operating cash flow improved by 23%, underscoring our disciplined execution in a highly dynamic environment." Ferguson noted strong double-digit sales growth in Metal Coatings, driven by higher volumes and infrastructure projects, while also acknowledging margin pressure due to increased exposure to solar and transmission distribution markets.
  • Ferguson highlighted that Precoat Metals encountered mixed market conditions due to tariffs and softer demand in building construction, HVAC, and appliances, but emphasized "Precoat benefited from the tariff impact on prepainted imported metal" and continues to achieve new customer wins and market share gains, especially in the aluminum container market.
  • Ferguson discussed the ramp-up at the new Washington, Missouri facility, stating, "sales continued to increase, and operating leverage is improving as we ramp up production. We remain confident in achieving gross margin improvements as volumes grow at the new site through the second half of the year."
  • Ferguson outlined continued investment in proprietary technology, including Oracle migration, AI exploration, and new galvanizing processes. He also detailed the integration of the Ohio facility with their digital systems.
  • Jason Crawford, CFO, reported, "for the second quarter, we reported sales of $417.3 million, representing a 2% increase from $409 million in the prior year period." Crawford noted that Metal Coatings sales increased 10.8%, while Precoat Metals sales declined 4.3%. He stated, "second quarter gross profit was $101.3 million or 24.3% of sales compared to $103.5 million or 25.3% of sales in the same quarter of the prior year."
  • Crawford added that interest expense for the quarter was $13.7 million, a reduction of $8.2 million from the prior year, attributing the improvement to debt paydown, debt repricing, and a new accounts receivable securitization facility. He also stated, "reported net income for the second quarter was $89.3 million."
  • Crawford reported an adjusted net income of $46.9 million, or adjusted diluted EPS of $1.55, compared to $1.37 in the prior year, a 13.1% increase. Adjusted EBITDA was $88.7 million or 21.3% of sales.

Outlook

  • Ferguson reaffirmed, "for our 2026 fiscal year, we are reiterating guidance for total sales, EBITDA and adjusted EPS. We anticipate that our sales will be in a range of $1.625 billion to $1.725 billion. Adjusted EBITDA will be within the lower half of the range of $360 million to $400 million due to the lack of Avail equity income as they continue to transition without the Electrical Products businesses. Adjusted diluted earnings per share will be in a range of $5.75 to $6.25, which translates to an increase of between 10% to 20% over the fiscal 2025 adjusted earnings."
  • Ferguson noted, "although markets may be choppy in the second half of our current fiscal year, which extends through February 2026, our numbers are supported by strength in projects and structural steel demand forecast."

Financial Results

  • Crawford reported that operating income for the quarter was $68.5 million or 16.4% of sales, compared to $67.6 million or 16.5% of sales in the prior year quarter.
  • Cash flow from operations was $58.4 million, and capital expenditures totaled $19.3 million. Acquisition spending for the new Canton, Ohio galvanizing facility was $30.1 million. Dividend payments increased over the prior year.
  • The net leverage ratio remained at 1.7x, reflecting ongoing debt reduction efforts.

Q&A

  • Ghansham Panjabi, Baird, inquired about Precoat market share gains and the impact of tariffs. Ferguson responded that "we picked up share gain because the -- and we referenced it, the prepainted imports are because of the tariffs are down significantly. So that's been transitioning to domestic supply, and we're painting at least as much as our share. But if you take that, it's probably, David, what about 10% on imports. So we've been picking up our share of it. So we picked up 3% or 4% to offset the roughly 9%, 10% market decline."
  • Panjabi further asked about Precoat segment volumes and offsets. Ferguson and Crawford cited sustained market share gains, ongoing ramp at Washington, and readiness to capitalize on any construction rebound.
  • Nick Giles, B. Riley Securities, asked about guidance range drivers and Washington facility contribution. Ferguson pointed to the lack of Avail EBITDA as the main impact and anticipated interest savings, while Crawford detailed the Washington ramp, noting it was "very much in line with expectations."
  • Timna Tanners, Wells Fargo, queried about the import opportunity's stage. Ferguson replied, "It's really early innings. I think probably a couple of months of that. So that should have a good tail to it."
  • Adam Thalhimer, Thompson, Davis, asked about tariffs' dual impact on Precoat. David Nark, CMO, explained that while prepainted imports were down 23%, the bare Galvalume market was down about 50%, offsetting some gains.
  • Mark Reichman, NOBLE Capital, asked about interest expense and SG&A expectations. Crawford stated improvement in interest expense would continue and "that 8% number is fairly representative" for SG&A through year-end.
  • Jon Braatz, Kansas City Capital, inquired about Canton acquisition revenue, to which Ferguson answered, "revenues in the quarter, $2 million, yes, and a few hundred thousand of contribution margin."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts' questions focused on segment-specific market share, tariff effects, guidance range, M&A prospects, and facility ramp-ups. The tone was neutral to slightly positive, with analysts acknowledging solid results while probing for clarity on guidance and market dynamics.
  • Management maintained a confident and measured tone during both prepared remarks and Q&A, reiterating guidance and highlighting operational execution. Ferguson and Crawford provided detailed explanations, with Ferguson stating, "we remain confident in achieving gross margin improvements as volumes grow at the new site through the second half of the year."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, sentiment remained steady, with a continued emphasis on disciplined execution and cautious optimism regarding market headwinds.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for fiscal 2026 was reiterated, with no upward revision since Q1. Management continues to target sales of $1.625 billion to $1.725 billion, adjusted EBITDA in the lower half of $360 million to $400 million, and adjusted diluted EPS of $5.75 to $6.25.
  • Metal Coatings continued to deliver double-digit sales growth, while Precoat faced ongoing mixed conditions but maintained margin discipline and gained share from reduced imports.
  • Management's tone remained confident, highlighting operational strength and strategic positioning despite market challenges. Analysts' focus was consistent, with questions centered on tariffs, market share, and facility performance.
  • Financial performance metrics, including EPS growth and margin profiles, showed resilience despite a slight decline in overall sales and margin mix compared to Q1. The net leverage ratio remained stable, reflecting disciplined capital allocation.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited ongoing tariff impacts, customer hesitancy on non-infrastructure projects, and softness in construction, HVAC, and appliance end markets as continuing challenges.
  • Strategies to mitigate these risks include maintaining cost discipline, pursuing market share gains, ramping new facility volumes, and targeting infrastructure-driven demand.
  • Analysts expressed concerns about the potential for further losses from the Avail JV, but management forecasted zero equity earnings from unconsolidated subsidiaries for the remainder of the year.

Final Takeaway

Management highlighted strong execution and disciplined capital allocation as key drivers for continued growth. With robust infrastructure demand fueling Metal Coatings, operational improvements, and ongoing ramp-up at the Washington facility, AZZ remains confident in its ability to achieve fiscal 2026 targets despite tariff-related and construction market headwinds. The company’s strategic focus on technology upgrades, organic investments, and bolt-on M&A positions it to sustain value creation for stakeholders.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Sea Limited falls after six sessions of gain
data.description:
Sea Limited (NYSE:SE) shares fell 3.64% to $185.68 in the afternoon trading on Thursday, breaking a six-session streak of gains. 

The stock remains down 2.99% over the past month, but has surged 77.95% year-to-date, significantly outperforming broader market indices.

Investors remain positive about the company's growth prospects, with Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating assigning a "Hold" recommendation for Sea, with an average score of 3.28 out of 5. The company scores A+ in both growth and momentum, and A for profitability, though it earned a D- for valuation and a C for revisions.

Seeking Alpha analysts rate the stock a Buy with an average score of 3.87.

Wall Street sentiment remains broadly bullish, with 20 analysts rating the stock a Strong Buy and 5 analysts giving it a Buy rating. Seven analysts recommend holding the stock, reflecting cautious optimism about Sea's long-term growth potential despite current valuation concerns.

Analyst A.J. Button pointed out Sea Limited's strong market position in Southeast Asia, adding, "Shopee's position in Southeast Asian e-commerce is impressive," but cautioned that "increasing competition" and "a potential growth ceiling" could challenge the company's future performance outside its core market.

data.title: Meet the brands that teenagers like the most: TikTok, Roblox, Yeti, Alani Nu, Sephora, and more
data.description:
Piper Sandler Companies released the results of its 50th semi-annual Taking Stock With Teens survey on Thursday.

"This fall, teens self-reported annual spending dropped to $2,213, which is a 6% decline year-over year and a 1% decline on average in the past 10 years. On a positive note, looking at wallet share for the upper-income teen, clothing grew 1% year-over-year, driven by females, while footwear share held steady after a decline last spring," highlighted Piper Sandler analyst Anna Andreeva.

Nike (NYSE:NKE) once again was the top clothing brand for teens, with Hollister (ANF) ranking number two and Brandy Melville a distant third among upper income teens. In footwear, Nike (NYSE:NKE) was the top brand, followed by Adidas (OTCQX:ADDYY) and then New Balance. On Running (ONON) jumped to fourth place to displace HOKA (DECK).

In the beauty category, e.l.f. Cosmetics (ELF) continues to dominate as the top cosmetics brand among teens, with Selena Gomez's Rare Beauty brand a distant second. Sephora (OTCPK:LVMHF) remains the number one favorite beauty shopping destination, followed by Ulta Beauty (ULTA) at number two. 

Notably, Walmart (WMT) has steadily gained share in the apparel and footwear categories among upper-income female teens, although Target (TGT) remains the preferred shopping destination. In a breakout, Yeti (NYSE:YETI) achieved a record level of brandawareness this year.

Chick-fil-A held on to its position as the preferred chain restaurant among teens, while McDonald's (MCD) and Chipotle Mexican Grill (CMG) ranked number two and three. Dr. Pepper (KDP) remained the number one favorite beverage brand, followed by Coca-Cola (KO) and Gatorade (PEP). Lays (PEP) was the top choice among teens for snacks, followed by Goldfish (CPB) and Cheez-Its (K). Red Bull was the top energy drink brand in the survey, followed by Monster (MNST) and Alani Nu, now owned by Celsius Holdings (CELH).

No surprise, but TikTok was once again the favorite app of teens, and Netflix (NFLX) was the top choice for video consumption with teens. Of interest, Roblox (NYSE:RBLX) did not lose any steam this year and improved its mindshare with the teenager demographic.

Why does it matter? Analysts note that teenager spending moves the economic needle both  directly through billions spent annually and indirectly through the influence teens have on household purchase decisions and emerging market trends.

data.title: Keystone Global Financial files for IPO of 1.25M shares
data.description:
Keystone Global Financial Group has filed a preliminary prospectus for an initial public offering of 1.25M ordinary shares, representing 10% of the total issued and outstanding shares.

The Cayman Islands holding company provides financial and strategic advisory services to the infrastructure and construction industries.

The prospectus filed on Form F-1 with the Securities and Exchange Commission is not an offer to sell.

The IPO is expected to be priced between $4.00 and $6.00 per ordinary share, and the company plans to reserve KCG as its ticker symbol on the Nasdaq Capital Market.

data.title: Zenas BioPharma rises after Wedbush raises price target
data.description:
Shares of Zenas BioPharma (NASDAQ:ZBIO) rose on Thursday after Wedbush raised its price target, pointing to the company’s expanding late-stage pipeline and successful asset in-licensing strategy.

The price target for the company was raised to $40 from $35.

On Wednesday, the company in-licensed multiple assets from InnoCare Pharma, including orelabrutinib, a BTK inhibitor currently in phase 3 development for progressive forms of multiple sclerosis.

Wedbush analysts said that the addition of these assets strengthen Zenas’s growth outlook and adds meaningful diversification to its portfolio.

Wedbush also highlighted near-term clinical catalysts that could further enhance ZBIO’s valuation, including the anticipated Phase 3 INDIGO trial results for obexelimab in IgG4-related disease by year-end 2025 and Phase 2 MoonStone trial results in MS expected in early fourth-quarter 2025. 

Wedbus reiterated Outperform rating. The shares of the company are up about 3% today.

data.title: Scholar Rock wins Buy at BofA on blockbuster potential for lead drug
data.description:
Scholar Rock (NASDAQ:SRRK) traded higher on Thursday after Bank of America initiated its coverage with a Buy recommendation, citing a blockbuster sales potential for the biotech’s lead asset, apitegromab, targeted at a rare neuromuscular disease called spinal muscular atrophy (SMA).

Analyst Tazeen Ahmad’s bullish view came despite the U.S. FDA’s recent decision to decline marketing authorization for apitegromab in SMA due to issues at a third-party fill-finish facility.

Ahmad noted that the setback marked a delay rather than a risk to the drug’s approval, and the agency is expected to greenlight it following a potential resubmission of the market application once Scholar Rock's (NASDAQ:SRRK) manufacturing partner resolves the regulatory concerns.

Noting that all three SMA drugs have each generated more than $1B in sales last year, the analyst projected $2B in risk-adjusted peak sales for apitegromab in 2037, making up $44 of her $53 per share target on Scholar Rock (NASDAQ:SRRK).

“While approved SMA therapies (Spinraza, Evrysdi, Zolgensma) reduce progressive motor neuron loss, significant unmet need remains, as these are not curative and disease progression persists,” Ahmad argued.

The analyst added that apitegromab is the first SMA therapy to show a positive effect on the disease, and it can address a key symptom of the disease, as it is designed to inhibit myostatin protein, which breaks down muscle.

data.title: Fox Entertainment buys stake in vertical video producer Holywater
data.description:
Fox Entertainment, a division of Fox Corporation (NASDAQ:FOX) (FOXA), has invested in vertical video company Holywater to capitalize on an $8B industry as well as the popularity of TikTok, Instagram (META), and YouTube Shorts (GOOG) (GOOGL).

Vertical videos is a format where the height of the video is greater than the width and meant to be viewed vertically on a smartphone. The videos are commonly viewed on social media platforms.

With over 55M users, Holywater operates platforms such as My Drama, My Passion, and My Muse, and airs popular dramas like The Shy Beauty and the Billionaire Beast, and Pregnant Cinderella, according to Deadline.

As part of Fox’s (NASDAQ:FOX) (FOXA) investment, the entertainment studio will create and produce more than 200 vertical videos for My Drama over the next two years.

“Innovation in digital storytelling is shaping the future of entertainment and investing in vertical video positions Fox Entertainment to achieve sustained, long-term growth as we build a modern studio for the future,” said Rob Wade, CEO of Fox Entertainment.

The amount of Fox’s (NASDAQ:FOX) (FOXA) investment was not disclosed.

The development did little to stem the pressure on Fox (FOX) (FOXA) shares, which continue to drift lower for the seventh straight day.

data.title: Gold surges on de-dollarization, debasement, and diversification – analyst
data.description:
Gold (XAUUSD:CUR) and other metals are experiencing significant upward momentum driven by three key factors: de-dollarization, debasement concerns, and investor diversification, according to Jeff Currie, chief strategy officer of energy pathways at Carlyle.

In an interview with CNBC, Currie noted that central banks now hold 27% of their reserves in gold, exceeding their holdings in U.S. Treasury securities, as part of a de-dollarization trend that has been ongoing for more than five years.

“We can add in the debasement trade, which really started to gain steam with the government shutdown, that we saw the ETF have 110 tons of inflows recently around the debasement,” he said.

The third factor driving metals is diversification, as investors seek alternatives to tight credit spreads in fixed income and stretched equity markets.

For individual investors looking to capitalize on the metals rally, Currie recommends ETFs (GLD), (IAU), (SGOL), (OUNZ), (BAR) as an excellent investment vehicle, particularly for gold.

“The ETF is an excellent way to play it because it’s one of the few ETFs in commodities where you actually own the physical bars,” he said.

Copper (HG1:COM) presents what Currie describes as a “perfect storm” of conditions, with prices up nearly 25% year-to-date. The industrial metal faces long-term underinvestment challenges while experiencing unexpected demand surges from AI data centers and defense-related needs, all atop the ongoing electrification trend. Supply issues at three major mines in Indonesia, Chile, and the Democratic Republic of Congo have further tightened the market, creating a deficit situation.

Despite the broader commodity momentum, crude oil (CL1:COM) has remained under pressure due to concerns about a supply glut. However, Currie emphasized that this anticipated oversupply has not significantly impacted prices, with Brent Crude (CO1:COM) maintaining levels around $65-66 per barrel.

“If it was going to impact prices, it would have already impacted prices,” he noted, adding that market conditions suggest refineries will likely process rather than store available oil.

Below are commodities ETFs:

  • Gold and Gold Miner ETFs: (GLD), (IAU), (SGOL), (OUNZ), (BAR), (GDX), (GDXJ), (NUGT), (RING), and (DUST).
  • Silver and Silver Mining ETFs: (SLV), (SIVR), (AGQ), (ZSL), (PSLV), (SIL), (SILJ), and (SLVP).
  • Copper and Copper Mining ETFs: (CPER), (COPX), and (OTC:JJCTF).
  • Oil ETFs: (USO), (UCO), (DBO), (OILK), and (USL).
  • Natural Gas ETFs: (UNG), (BOIL), and (UNL)
